general: imagery should have high appetite appeal
photograph silo images consistently according to packaging or product type
minimize reflections on plastic, foil, and shiny packaging, especially when reflections obscure product information
remove unnecessary stickers that are not part of the package design
present packaging and labels straight rather than angled or crooked
photograph each unique and meaningful side of the product or package
for round or irregular shapes, center printed information and graphics and keep them legible
treat the side with predominant branding, labeling, format, variant, and size or quantity as the front view
light products evenly from side to side and top to bottom
ensure exposure and color correctly represent the product
maintain separation between the product and background
clip silo products onto pure white RGB 255/255/255
retouch dirt, dust, fingerprints, damage, blemishes, expiration dates, lot numbers, and sample-only information
use selective focus only when it keeps imagery uncluttered and easy to understand
keep both the product and its packaging in focus when both appear in the same image
make lifestyle imagery realistic, professionally lit, and appropriate to the item and brand
keep the product being sold as the hero of lifestyle imagery
incorporate packaging into lifestyle imagery when practical
display every required and conditionally required image and meet the minimum image count indicated by the source page
individual brand guidelines take precedence

main_image.label: Main Image
main_image.description: Primary carousel hero image for the PDP.

silo_front.label: Silo, Front
silo_front.guidance: Show the product isolated on pure white in a straight-on hero view or the cleanest angle needed to communicate its form.
silo_front.recommendation: Add a clean front hero image on pure white showing the complete product or set.

lifestyle_in_use.label: Lifestyle, In-Use
lifestyle_in_use.guidance: Show the product assembled, installed, worn, or used in a believable environment so its activity, fit, scale, and intended use are clear.
lifestyle_in_use.recommendation: Add a realistic in-use image showing the product in its intended sport, activity, fit, or installed context.

feature_graphic.label: Feature Graphic
feature_graphic.guidance: Use a clean infographic or detail image to communicate performance, safety, compatibility, materials, included parts, mechanisms, construction, or another important feature.
feature_graphic.recommendation: Add a clean feature graphic highlighting the most important specification, benefit, included component, or functional detail.

silo_in_pack.label: Silo, In Pack
silo_in_pack.guidance: Show the customer-facing retail packaging, primary carton, ship pack, delivery pack, crate, or transport state when reasonable for the product.
silo_in_pack.recommendation: Add a clear image of the packaging or delivered transport state the customer receives.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.guidance: Show dimensions, size, fit, compatibility, clearance, setup footprint, regulation information, or other relevant physical specifications clearly.
dimensions.recommendation: Add a clean dimensions or specification graphic showing relevant size, fit, compatibility, or setup measurements.

additional_recommendations: If foldable or portable, add a collapsed or transport view.
If capacity, player count, or weight limit matters, add that guidance.
If accessories are included, show them clearly.
Add an alternate angle, storage or transport view, or assembly or installation graphic when useful.
notes: Silo Front should show the full product in the angle that best communicates size, profile, and overall form.
Lifestyle should show the product assembled or in believable use context so footprint, scale, and intended activity are clear.
One feature graphic should call out the most important performance, safety, capacity, or included-feature story.
The other feature graphic should show key included parts, setup steps, or a mechanism detail.
A three-quarter hero is acceptable when straight-on does not explain depth well.
If retail packaging is not reasonable, Silo In Pack may become a delivery, crate, or transport view.

additional_recommendations: If multiple diameters, lengths, weights, or compatibility ranges apply, add a fit chart.
If sold as a multipack, add a count graphic.
If included tools matter, show them.
Add a back-of-pack label, alternate angle, or installation graphic when useful.
notes: Silo Front should use a straight-on hero view or the cleanest angle for the part profile.
Lifestyle may show fit, installed, or use context.
One feature graphic should communicate compatibility, sizing, caliber, or the most important specification.
The other feature graphic should show the connection point, material, or most important functional detail.
Dimensions should show product size, fit, or compatibility ranges.
Use the lifestyle slot for fit or installed context when a traditional lifestyle image is not useful.
